Claim: The Phase-1 ceiling is structural under the tested listing/window/local-rewrite model: Phase-1 optimizers achieve ~0% gate reduction when no listing-adjacent inverse/mergeable pairs are exposed.
Limitations_Caveats: Not a universal lower bound. Ceiling is listing/window/model-conditional; WCL validation confirmed for random Universal family (C9).

Claim: Phase-2a commutation rewriting provides context-dependent improvement on select circuit families.
Limitations_Caveats: Improvement is family-dependent. Phase-2a empirical results do not prove Phase-2b template-assisted theorems.

Claim: Production compiler gap analysis reveals mechanisms beyond the prototype peephole model.
Limitations_Caveats: Hardware-specific effects not fully modeled. Cirq/t|ket> comparisons are present in E20 but require independent verification.

Claim: Phase-2a window size w affects the trade-off between optimization power and runtime.
Limitations_Caveats: Underpowered comparison (reported power=0.126); treat saturation as empirical trend not confirmed design rule.

Claim: Hardware topology constraints affect optimization opportunities differently.
Limitations_Caveats: Simplified topology models. Real hardware has additional noise/error effects not modeled.

Claim: Clifford+T gate-set decomposition preserves some Phase-2 value among surviving rows.
Limitations_Caveats: Survivorship-biased: decomposition/fidelity failures are excluded. Do not generalize to failed rows or full attempted population.

Claim: Exact functionality is preserved where fidelity is computed and retained.
Limitations_Caveats: Exact/scalable unitary comparison for supported qubit sizes. Failed/invalid rows are filtered where valid fidelity is required.

Claim: The Phase-1 reduction ceiling is formally established for bounded circuits with the bounded INSERTION cascade gap resolved.
Limitations_Caveats: The unbounded INSERTION cascade remains open. QMA-hardness of CODP remains separate theoretical motivation.

Claim: Listing-model dependency: WCL-style preprocessing exposes Phase-1 opportunities hidden under LBL.
Limitations_Caveats: Cross-family WCL validation (beyond random Universal) remains future work; Phase-2 commutation opportunities under WCL not separately measured.

Claim: Ceiling-aware optimization can skip futile optimization passes with identical reduction and significant speedup.
Limitations_Caveats: Exact fidelity verified only for n<=6; larger circuits use target=None (correctness is covered by unit tests).

Claim: Multi-compiler extension with Cirq and t|ket> produces comparable reduction and runtime data.
Limitations_Caveats: Baseline compiler settings are standardized but may not reflect optimal per-family tuning.

Claim: Phase-2b template-assisted BV advantage is theoretically proved and the required template is implemented and unit-tested.
Limitations_Caveats: Do not conflate Phase-2b theorem/tests with E10/E11/E14/E16 Phase-2a canonical results. Full canonical-scale Phase-2b benchmark rerun remains future work.

Claim: Clifford circuits in Aaronson-Gottesman canonical form have empty Phase-1 action space (R1=0).
Limitations_Caveats: Validation uses random AG canonical circuits; does not cover all possible canonical forms exhaustively.

Claim: Explicit artificial circuit family with R1=0 and R_{1+2a} >= 1/6.
Limitations_Caveats: Construction uses even n>=4; odd n and minimal n=2 not tested.
